title: Sports Against Crime Netball Tourney held with pride
description: MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Members of the KwaNonqaba...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Members of the KwaNonqaba Police and the KwaNonqaba CPF celebrated Women's Day by hosting a Sports Against Crime Netball Tournament for several primary schools in the greater Mossel Bay area on Saturday 9 August.","html":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Members of the KwaNonqaba Police and the KwaNonqaba CPF celebrated Women's Day by hosting a Sports Against Crime Netball Tournament for several primary schools in the greater Mossel Bay area on Saturday 9 August."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The tournament, held in Herbertsdale, saw teams from Isalathiso Primary, Garden Route Primary, Global Academy of Education and Herbertsdale Primary compete for the coveted trophy."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The organisers said the idea was to bring a tournament to the rural areas and expose the schools to tougher competition.","html":"The organisers said the idea was to bring a tournament to the rural areas and expose the schools to tougher competition."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"KwaNonqaba CPF's Otto Ploog and the rest of the team opened the proceedings with a message of hope and pride for women and girls.","html":"KwaNonqaba CPF's Otto Ploog and the rest of the team opened the proceedings with a message of hope and pride for women and girls."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"They were encouraged not to keep quiet in the face of domestic violence and to speak up when others cannot.","html":"They were encouraged not to keep quiet in the face of domestic violence and to speak up when others cannot."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Soup was handed out to the Herbertsdale community following the tournament. Photo: Supplied","html":"Soup was handed out to the Herbertsdale community following the tournament. Photo: Supplied"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The tournament itself saw some tough and fast-paced matches, and each school supported its team with pride. The winners, however, were Isalathiso's team. Garden Route placed second and Herbertsdale came in third.","html":"The tournament itself saw some tough and fast-paced matches, and each school supported its team with pride. The winners, however, were Isalathiso's team. Garden Route placed second and Herbertsdale came in third."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Following the tournament, KwaNon-qaba's Community in Blue and the CPF went into the Herbertsdale community to dish out some soup to the residents.","html":"Following the tournament, KwaNon-qaba's Community in Blue and the CPF went into the Herbertsdale community to dish out some soup to the residents."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The team was led by KwaNonqaba's rural sector commander, Warrant Officer JC Brink. The organisers thanked all the sponsors who helped make the day a success.","html":"The team was led by KwaNonqaba's rural sector commander, Warrant Officer JC Brink. The organisers thanked all the sponsors who helped make the day a success."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Some of the players with their teachers, and members of the KwaNonqaba Police and the KwaNonqaba CPF. Photo: Supplied","html":"Some of the players with their teachers, and members of the KwaNonqaba Police and the KwaNonqaba CPF. Photo: Supplied"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
